about.content:
I began my programming journey in high school about eight years ago, focusing on UI design and frontend development. Over the past two years of my professional career, I have expanded my skills in backend and database management, becoming more adaptable and eager to learn.
Through both university studies and professional work, I have gained strong teamwork experience — actively engaging in code reviews, project planning, and team discussions. My four years as a tutor have also strengthened my communication and teaching skills.
I am a fast learner who enjoys collaboration and continuous self-improvement, aiming to create projects that combine functionality with excellent user experience.

items.title: QA Intern – Codibly S.A.
items.description: My first job in the IT sector, completed as part of a high school internship program. It was a period of intensive learning, during which I gained insight into teamwork dynamics and the process of software development and testing. I participated in training sessions, project meetings, and daily tasks, gradually improving my technical skills and understanding of industry best practices.
responsibilities: Conducting stress tests using JMeter
Writing tests for frontend components
Performing manual unit tests and reporting results
Participating in training sessions on software testing methodologies
Attending team meetings focused on work organization and task planning
Developing simple web applications to enhance technical skills

items.title: Technical Assistant – Aptiv Services Poland
items.description: The second internship completed as part of my high school program, this time with a stronger focus on working with network systems and their physical maintenance. I was responsible for assembling integrated circuits, providing technical support to senior staff, and developing simple Python scripts for testing closed network systems.
responsibilities: Maintenance of physical network and electronic infrastructure
Installation and testing of integrated circuits and hardware components
Providing helpdesk and on-site technical support
Developing simple Python scripts for testing and diagnostics
Troubleshooting and performing minor hardware repairs

items.title: Tutor – Edualy
items.description: During my work as a tutor, I developed strong communication skills, patience, and an individualized approach to students. I learned how to effectively explain complex concepts in an accessible way, foster motivation, and support the development of analytical thinking and independent problem-solving skills.
responsibilities: Preparing students for final exams in computer science, mathematics, and physics
Conducting lessons for vocational exam preparation
Providing academic-level tutoring in web technologies, algorithms, and data structures

items.title: Junior Software Developer – Codibly
items.description: As a member of the development team, I participated in the implementation of commercial projects in the field of e-mobility and renewable energy. My work involved designing and developing solutions for both client-side and server-side applications, as well as actively contributing to planning, requirements analysis, and process optimization within the team. I collaborated directly with clients, presenting project progress and proposing functional improvements, while also providing UX/UI design advice. Additionally, I supported the recruitment process of interns and continuously expanded my technical skills by learning new technologies.
responsibilities: Designing and implementing frontend and backend functionalities
Collaborating with the project team on requirement refinement and sprint planning
Maintaining and extending existing system modules
Participating in daily Scrum meetings and code reviews
Assisting in the recruitment of interns and supporting their technical growth
Regularly presenting project progress and deliverables to the client
Learning and adopting new technologies in response to project needs
Providing active input on design and UX/UI aspects

items.title: Node.js Developer – Solidstudio
items.description: As a member of the technical support team, I worked on maintaining and improving the company’s core product, which delivers e-mobility solutions to clients worldwide. I was responsible for direct communication with clients, verifying and resolving reported issues, and implementing new functionalities based on their requirements. Additionally, I contributed to the development of an AI-based tool aimed at automating the most time-consuming aspects of ticket evaluation and handling.
responsibilities: Fixing bugs and implementing features based on client requests
Direct communication with clients and participation in requirement planning and analysis
Acting as a technical expert in the field of e-mobility protocols
Regular monitoring of infrastructure status and responding to accuring issues
Developing an AI-based tool aimed at optimizing the handling of client support tickets

githubCard.title: View more on GitHub
githubCard.subtitle: Explore all my repositories and side projects.

items.name: Weather App
items.description:
The Weather App is a full-stack application that provides weather forecasts, along with estimates of energy generated by photovoltaic panels for the upcoming week, based on the user's geolocation.
Frontend is a React with Material-UI, which utilizes my Node.js endpoint. The application also offers a dark mode feature.

items.name: Color Lines
items.description:
Project is a replica of the widely available Color Lines game (match 5).
From a technical standpoint, it involves a grid of div elements generated at random positions with randomly colored balls. Win condition is based on the number of balls on the board, and the core of the game is the Depth-First Search (DFS) algorithm for pathfinding. The project is essentially 100% TypeScript

items.name: Bot Shooter
items.description:
An <orange>AI-driven</orange> arena shooter built with <orange>Pygame</orange>, where bots navigate, detect enemies, and engage in combat. The goal is to reach 5 kills as quickly as possible. 

Bots compete in a closed arena, collecting resources and eliminating opponents. Their behavior is driven by a <orange>state machine</orange>, and the map is represented as a graph with <orange>A* pathfinding</orange> implementation.

items.name: Physics Simulation
items.description: This project is a collection of interactive <orange>physics simulations</orange> created using <orange>pygame</orange>. It includes implementations of systems such as <orange>mass-spring models</orange>, <orange>cloth simulation</orange> with <orange>Verlet integration</orange>, and <orange>collision mechanics</orange> for soft and rigid bodies — accompanied by visual representations and detailed mathematical formulas included in the README.

items.name: Mp3 Player
items.description:
A project fulfilling the role of a music player with an interface and the capability of uploading custom playlists.
The majority of the project is centered around the NodeJS backend, which handles the reading of music albums from the internal project folder and the storage of user-uploaded albums. On the frontend side, a simple CSS with additional animations is employed.

items.name: Snake Game
items.description:
Snake Game is essentially my first encounter withprogramming, created on Client-Side Application classes on my first year in highschool.
The project is based on a simple interval with 'movement' of the snake, randomly generates the position of the fruit and uses a straightforward collision mechanism. I must admit that its presence here is primarily due to nostalgia
